items.title: Health Ministry Threatens Enforcement Action Against Doctor Fee-Splitting
items.summary: MOH pledges action under Act 586 against doctor fee-splitting based on proper investigation and legal assessment. As for TPAs and insurers involved in fee-splitting under discount or panel arrangements, MOH says it may engage the relevant authorities. The post Health Ministry Threatens Enforcement Action Against Doctor Fee-Splitting appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Government Should Lease A Private Hospital In PJ
items.summary: Instead of building a general hospital, the government should lease a medium-sized private hospital in PJ and take over its entire operations as a public hospital. This would immediately increase public hospital capacity in PJ and reduce pressure on UMMC. The post Government Should Lease A Private Hospital In PJ appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Patient Communication Is A Clinical Duty, Not A Political Battleground — PMPASKL
items.summary: In medical practice, communication is part of clinical responsibility. Ensuring that patients understand health instructions does not weaken respect for the national language. It reflects competence, compassion, and sound risk management. The post Patient Communication Is A Clinical Duty, Not A Political Battleground — PMPASKL appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: When Sungai Buloh Hospital KPIs Matter More Than Human Limits — Medical Officer
items.summary: Sungai Buloh Hospital's Internal Medicine is expected to function like several departments. 40 MOs manage 4 major medical wards, lodger and MDSU services, medical admission ward, CCU, plus multiple general medical and geriatric wards at Pusat Kusta Negara. The post When Sungai Buloh Hospital KPIs Matter More Than Human Limits — Medical Officer appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: ProtectHealth Improves GP Benefits, Targets 40-Something Adults To Boost Take-Up
items.summary: ProtectHealth has raised GP fees and doubled follow-up payments under Peka B40. Despite providing free health screenings, take-up rates are very low at 27%. Hence, ProtectHealth plans to target adults in their 40s by appealing to family responsibility. The post ProtectHealth Improves GP Benefits, Targets 40-Something Adults To Boost Take-Up appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: A 680km Journey To Save Young Lives
items.summary: Sepeda Amal Borneo 2026 calls on public support to help fund life-saving surgeries and care for children facing life-threatening and often overlooked medical conditions. The post A 680km Journey To Save Young Lives appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Motherhood, Recovery, And The Invisible Labour We Refuse To See — June Joseph
items.summary: For many women, postpartum is marked instead by prolonged physical depletion, emotional strain, and sustained caregiving labour that continues well beyond discharge from hospital. The post Motherhood, Recovery, And The Invisible Labour We Refuse To See — June Joseph appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Newcastle University Claims Malaysian Medical Graduates Can Still Work In UK
items.summary: Newcastle University claims it's still possible for NUMed graduates to work in the UK because NUMed's MBBS degree remains recognised. "It is important, however, to distinguish between changes in workforce policy and the academic value of a medical degree." The post Newcastle University Claims Malaysian Medical Graduates Can Still Work In UK appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Why A 2+1 Schedule For PCV20 Is The Smart Choice — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin, Dr Zulkifli Ismail & Dr David Ng
items.summary: Public health policy should be guided not only by licensing pathways, but also by evolving immunological and real-world effectiveness data. The post Why A 2+1 Schedule For PCV20 Is The Smart Choice — Dr Musa Mohd Nordin, Dr Zulkifli Ismail & Dr David Ng appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Reflections For World Schizophrenia Day — Dr Loshi Rajen
items.summary: With appropriate treatment and support, people with schizophrenia can and do live meaningful lives, but many never receive the conditions needed for that to unfold. The post Reflections For World Schizophrenia Day — Dr Loshi Rajen appeared first on CodeBlue .
